meta.description: Google Chrome browser. Multi-tab, CDP-based automation via BrowserWin.exe. Supports page navigation, DOM interaction, clipboard, screenshots, and JS execution.

Chrome is automated via BrowserWin.exe using the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). There are NO UIA AutomationIds for browser content — all element targeting uses CSS selectors, XPath, or semantic role queries via CDP / accessibility tree.
Entry point: LISTBROWSERS target="SYSTEM" → list running browser instances (chrome/edge/firefox) LAUNCH target="chrome" → launch Chrome (or attach); returns tab list NEWPAGE target="chrome" → open a blank new tab; returns PAGE:<id>
Per-tab operations (use PAGE:<id> or chrome:TITLE:<text>): NAVIGATE parameter="https://..." navigate to URL READ parameter="" read body text (innerText) READ parameter="css:#id" read element text or value SENDKEYS parameter="css:#inp|text" focus element by selector, then type CLICKID parameter="css:#btn" click element by CSS selector EXECUTE parameter="<js code>" run JS; returns serialized result QUERYTREE parameter="" returns DOM accessibility/snapshot tree SCREENSHOT parameter="" returns base64 PNG of full tab CLOSEPAGE target="PAGE:<id>" close a specific tab
Selector syntax for parameter: "css:<sel>" CSS selector e.g. "css:#username" "css:<sel>|<text>" focus by sel, type text "xpath:<expr>" XPath expression "role:<role>" ARIA role e.g. "role:button[Find]" "" no specific element (page-level for READ/SENDKEYS)
Keyboard shortcuts (via SENDKEYS with no element / window-level): {CTRL+T} new tab {CTRL+W} close tab {CTRL+L} focus address bar {CTRL+R} reload {F5} reload {ESCAPE} cancel/dismiss {TAB} next field {ENTER} submit {F12} DevTools {CTRL+F} find in page {ALT+LEFT} back {ALT+RIGHT} forward

Control.label: The Chrome browser process. Target: "chrome".
Use for browser-level operations: LAUNCH, LISTBROWSERS, NEWPAGE. The response to NEWPAGE includes a PAGE:<id> token for further tab operations.

action.note: launch or attach; returns tab list
open tab at URL; returns PAGE:id

Control.label: A single Chrome tab. Target: PAGE:<tabId>.

All content operations target a specific tab via PAGE:<id>. Use NAVIGATE to load a URL. Use QUERYTREE for DOM snapshot (returns accessibility tree — limited depth). Use READ for visible text. Use EXECUTE for arbitrary JS.

Control.note: Chrome address / omnibox bar — not in DOM; use SENDKEYS {CTRL+L}
Control.label: Chrome address bar (omnibar).
Not a DOM element. Focus with {CTRL+L} via SENDKEYS on the tab. Then type URL and press {ENTER} to navigate. Alternatively use NAVIGATE command directly (preferred).
Generic text input field on a web page.

Target: use specific CSS selector or id for the actual input. SENDKEYS with "css:#fieldId|text" focuses and types. Use CLICKID first if the field needs a click to activate (e.g. custom dropdowns). Check with EXECUTE: document.querySelector('#id').value to verify.
Form submit button.
Most forms: CLICKID "css:button[type=submit]" or SENDKEYS {ENTER} on focused input. Verify submission by READ or checking URL change via EXECUTE: window.location.href.
The page body — all visible text content.

READ with no parameter returns body.innerText (all visible text). For specific sections use READ with css: selector. For structured data use EXECUTE: JSON.stringify(…) to return arrays/objects. innerText includes only visible text; use innerHTML or textContent via EXECUTE for more.
